title: Internet-facing asset — baseline hardening
content: For internet-facing systems: enforce TLS 1.2+, remove default accounts, enable centralized logging, and segment DMZ from internal VLANs. Require WAF or reverse-proxy rules for OWASP Top 10 patterns. Schedule monthly vulnerability scans and track time-to-patch for critical CVEs.

title: Brute-force and credential stuffing
content: After repeated login failures: enable account lockout with backoff, deploy MFA for all external identities, consider CAPTCHA on public login, and correlate source IPs with threat intel. Temporarily throttle IPs at the edge and notify users of password-reset only via verified channels.

title: Malware or suspicious execution signals
content: Isolate affected hosts from the network while preserving disk for forensics. Run EDR full scan, collect process tree and network connections, and rotate credentials used on that host. Escalate to incident commander if lateral movement indicators appear.

title: Port scan and reconnaissance
content: Tune IDS/IPS thresholds for scan bursts; block scanner IPs at the perimeter for 24–72h. Review exposed services on targeted subnets and close unnecessary ports. Document source ASNs for possible follow-up with ISP or cloud abuse teams.

title: High anomaly score (behavioral)
content: Elevated unsupervised anomaly scores warrant human review: compare against change calendar, verify whether traffic volume or auth patterns match a known release. If unexplained, treat as potential compromise and widen search to peer assets in the same tier.

title: High-value transactions
content: For transfers above policy thresholds: enforce dual control and out-of-band approver callback. Log immutable audit trail with user, device, and IP. Delay settlement until secondary verification completes; flag same-day changes to beneficiary accounts.

title: Failed or unauthorized payment attempts
content: Cluster failed attempts by user and beneficiary. Temporarily step up step-up auth for the user, review recent device changes, and block high-risk corridors until fraud ops clears. Notify treasury lead if aggregate failure rate spikes.

title: Restricted sensitivity payment systems
content: Payment servers with restricted sensitivity: minimize admin access, use jump hosts with session recording, and separate production keys from non-prod. Validate PAN/token flows never hit unintended logs. Quarterly access certification for break-glass accounts.

title: Success vs failure imbalance
content: When non-success share grows: reconcile gateway errors with bank files, check API rate limits and idempotency keys, and ensure customer-facing status pages stay accurate. Finance and IT should joint-review for systemic vs isolated causes.

title: Off-hours HR system access
content: Off-hours access to HR records: verify against on-call maintenance windows and approved overtime. Require manager attestation for bulk exports outside business hours. Add DLP alerts on large downloads and watermark sensitive reports.

title: Employee record export or bulk download
content: Exports containing PII must use encrypted channels, least-privilege roles, and purpose limitation documented in the ticket. For denied exports, capture justification in the audit log. Offer privacy office review when data crosses regions.

title: Salary or compensation data exposure risk
content: Treat compensation data as highest confidentiality: restrict query scopes, mask in non-prod, and alert on unusual query volumes. Executive briefing if CRITICAL risk band events tie to HR compensation objects.

title: HR confidential server posture
content: HR servers labeled confidential: no direct internet exposure, AV/EDR mandatory, and file integrity monitoring on policy tables. Integrate HRIS change logs with SIEM for modify/delete spikes.

title: Criticality level 4 assets
content: Criticality 4 assets require documented RTO/RPO, active/active or hot standby where affordable, and tabletop exercises twice yearly. Executive escalation path must be one hop from on-call to CIO/CISO for sustained outage.

title: High business value (score > 85)
content: When business_value exceeds 85 in the risk model: prioritize investment in resilience testing and chaos engineering. Board-level reporting if correlated CRITICAL logs exceed policy for two consecutive weeks.

title: Web server on IT department
content: Standard web tier: patch OS and frameworks weekly, use CSP headers, disable directory listing, and centralize TLS cert lifecycle. Pair SOC alerts for this asset with application-owner contact in the CMDB.

title: Cross-domain risk elevation
content: When average portfolio risk rises across SOC, Finance, and HR: convene a 30-minute executive triage. Ask each domain lead for top three root causes and one mitigation committed for the week. Document decisions in the risk register.

title: CRITICAL band concentration
content: Many CRITICAL-band events: open a virtual war room channel, freeze non-essential changes, and assign a single incident coordinator. Communicate externally only through comms-approved templates.

title: UNKNOWN risk band backlog
content: Large UNKNOWN risk_band counts often indicate legacy rows before scoring or ingestion gaps. Backfill scores where possible and exclude UNKNOWN from executive KPIs until below 5% of volume.

title: Internal vs external IP mix
content: Blend of internal and external IPs on auth failures: check for misconfigured proxies, split-horizon DNS issues, or VPN split tunneling. Validate NTP and SSO clock skew if Kerberos errors appear.

title: Flagged transactions in metadata
content: When metadata shows flagged=true: route to AML/fraud queue with enriched counterparty data. Do not auto-approve retries without human touch. Retain narrative for regulators if jurisdiction requires.

title: Denied HR actions
content: Spike in denied HR actions may indicate over-collected entitlements or attack probing. Review role mappings quarterly and implement just-in-time elevation for sensitive modify rights.

title: DDoS or volumetric anomaly
content: Volumetric anomalies: engage CDN scrubbing, announce affected prefixes if BGP-based mitigation exists, and preserve flow samples. Communicate customer impact via status page owner.

title: Withdrawal vs transfer mix
content: Shift toward withdrawals may signal liquidity stress or fraud. Treasury should validate intraday limits and correspondent cutoffs. Align with SOC if same users show concurrent auth anomalies.

title: Resource access count spike
content: Behavioral feature resource_access_count elevated: compare to role baseline. If tied to single user, interview for business justification; if many users, investigate integration batch job misconfiguration.

title: Internal exposure with high criticality
content: Even internal-only assets with high criticality need micro-segmentation: assume breach mindset, enforce east-west inspection on east-west traffic to the subnet, and restrict RDP/SSH sources.

title: Board-ready one-pager after incidents
content: Prepare a one-page brief: timeline, customer/data impact, containment actions, root cause hypothesis, and three preventive investments. Avoid jargon; quantify financial exposure ranges where legal allows.

title: Credential success after failures
content: Pattern of many failures then success from same IP: force password reset for affected accounts, invalidate refresh tokens, and check for new MFA device enrollment. Consider mandatory step-up for 72h.

title: Reconciliation breaks
content: Hold period-end close if reconciliation variance exceeds materiality. Trace to gateway vs ledger timing differences. Document compensating controls if manual adjustments are required.

title: Modify vs view ratio
content: High modify-to-view ratio on employee records can indicate mass correction or misuse. Require second pair of eyes for bulk modify and snapshot tables before bulk updates.

title: Vendor and third-party access
content: If risk correlates with vendor integration accounts: review OAuth scopes, rotate client secrets, and confirm vendor SOC2 controls map to your data classes.

title: SIEM rule tuning backlog
content: Reduce analyst fatigue: prioritize rules that fire only on chained behaviors, suppress known maintenance windows, and measure MTTD/MTTR weekly.

title: Payment server business value 95+
content: Highest-value payment assets: implement canary deployments, immutable backups, and cryptographic verification of release artifacts. CEO sign-off for any architecture drift.

title: HR server business value 70+
content: HR server with material business value: align retention schedules with legal holds, test restore quarterly, and map all integrations that copy PII out of HRIS.

title: Communication discipline during crises
content: Single narrative owner for external statements; legal reviews all regulatory filings; engineering avoids speculative root causes in public channels until confirmed.

title: Zero Trust alignment
content: Move toward continuous verification: device posture checks before resource access, short-lived tokens, and per-application segmentation instead of flat network trust.

title: Wire cutoff and human callback
content: Business email compromise defense: voice callback to a known number for beneficiary changes, dual approval for new payees, and banner warnings on emails containing wire instructions.

title: Workforce reduction or restructuring windows
content: During restructuring: increase monitoring on bulk exports, disable bulk APIs for non-HR roles, and offer secure channels for employee questions to reduce shadow IT.

title: Sensitivity restricted finance systems
content: Restricted sensitivity: classify data flows in architecture diagrams, enforce DLP on endpoints that can reach the subnet, and annual penetration test with remediation SLAs.

title: Metrics hygiene for executives
content: Executives should see risk trends with confidence intervals and data freshness timestamps. Exclude test environments from production KPIs and annotate major incidents on time-series charts.

title: Cloud identity anomalies
content: For cloud IdP: alert on impossible travel, new OAuth apps with broad scopes, and privilege role assignments outside change tickets. Automate revocation playbooks.

title: Intraday liquidity monitoring
content: Pair finance telemetry with market hours: set soft limits on cumulative outflows and auto-escalate to CFO when utilisation crosses policy corridors.

title: Integration service accounts
content: Service accounts touching HRIS: rotate keys quarterly, bind to fixed IP ranges, and log every read/write with correlation IDs to business process IDs.

title: Post-incident lessons learned
content: Within two weeks of major incidents: run blameless postmortem, file tracking tickets for action items, and tie at least one OKR to measurable risk reduction.
